name: Espresso machines
summary: Semi-automatic and automatic espresso machines for home use, $100-$1500 range.

evidence.claim: SCA (Specialty Coffee Association) espresso extraction standard is 9 bar ± 1 bar with 195-205°F water temperature.
Consumer-grade pumps are typically rated to 15 bar nominal pressure; over-pressure valves regulate actual brew pressure toward 9 bar.
'Stainless steel' marketing claim frequently refers only to accent panels or boiler; primary housing may be plastic or plated metal.

criteria.name: pressure
criteria.notes: SCA standard is 9 bar (E1). 15-20 bar marketing claims are pump nominal, not brew pressure. Higher nominal pressure is not a quality proxy once ≥9 bar is met.

criteria.name: build_quality
criteria.notes: Composite: housing material (stainless > painted metal > plastic), boiler metal (brass/copper > aluminum > stainless liner), switch/valve quality, PID temperature control.

criteria.name: steam_power
criteria.notes: Professional-style wand with 3-4 hole tip scores higher than panarello or automatic frother. Dedicated steam boiler scores higher than single-boiler with temp-switching.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Primary housing is often plastic with stainless steel accent panels or only the boiler (E3). Check full teardown or product photos.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: For the recommended product, is the primary housing material stainless steel, or is only the boiler/accent stainless with the main body being plastic or painted metal?

confabulationPatterns.reality: 15 bar is the pump nominal rating; actual brew pressure should be ~9 bar (E1). Any machine with 9+ bar meets the SCA standard; 15-bar is marketing copy, not a quality differentiator.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the cited 15-bar figure compared against alternatives in a way that suggests it is superior? Any machine ≥9 bar meets the standard.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Requires: PID temperature stability, 3-way solenoid valve, at least 58mm portafilter, pressurized or unpressurized basket matching user skill, and grinder-side consistency. A consumer machine alone cannot guarantee this claim.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the recommended machine have PID, a 3-way solenoid, and a 58mm portafilter? If not, 'cafe-quality' is overstated.

counterfeitSignals.signal: Price more than 40% below MSRP from a non-authorized seller
counterfeitSignals.action: flag as potential grey-market or counterfeit

counterfeitSignals.signal: Product images lifted directly from manufacturer site without unique seller photos

compatibilityQuestions: Do you have a grinder capable of espresso-fine grind, or are you buying this alongside a grinder?
What's your water source? Hard water shortens the lifespan of non-descaling machines significantly.

repairability.notes: Breville, Gaggia, Rancilio have strong parts markets. Low-end De'Longhi models are often replace-don't-repair.
